summary: Kenneth Suna argues that saving money in high-yield savings accounts (even at 4.5%) is insufficient for retirement and that investors must buy growth assets like dividend stocks. While he contrasts savings returns with stock market gains (McDonald's up 218% over 10 years plus dividends), this is general investing philosophy rather than actionable options or trading strategy for Brian's Premium Desk.
takeaways: Savings accounts at 4.5% cannot compound wealth into retirement
McDonald's stock delivered 218% appreciation plus 2.22% dividend yield over 10 years
Dividend growth can exceed current yield (McDonald's dividend grew 8.26% in 5 years)
High-yield savings rates will decline as interest rates fall in 2024
Emergency funds and short-term savings belong in HYSA, long-term wealth in stocks
title: Saving is not Investing #kennethsuna #investingforbeginners #simpleinvesting
